title: Determine the exact value of R(P10, P10, P10)
micro_paper_assessment: Full resolution would still be a strong micro-paper, but the current solve output is only a theorem-facing slice: a sharp K17 lower bound, an obstruction to one-vertex four-block extensions on K18, and bounded symmetric-family evidence. This is not yet paper-ready.
family_affinity: strong; first open diagonal three-color path case, with a reusable four-block extremal obstruction pattern for even path targets
generalization_signal: The one-vertex-extension obstruction of the standard four-block lower template appears to scale to even P_{2m}, but the stability reduction from arbitrary colorings is still missing.
candidate_theorem_slice: Every one-vertex extension of the standard (5,4,4,4) four-block lower-bound coloring for P10 already contains a monochromatic P10.
next_action: Continue the upper-bound route by proving a stability reduction from arbitrary K18 counterexamples to the four-block quotient pattern; keep the current theorem slice and K17 construction as support, not as a finished exact-value claim.
